blocks.speaker: Akein
blocks.content: Master! What happened? Did the experiment succeed?

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Yes, it succeeded! Our magic worked!
But Alpheas couldn't rejoice.
Erina's condition was clearly abnormal.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: But why is my wife like this? Darling! Snap out of it!
Akein rechecked the gauges and was shocked.
While individual metrics were normal, her overall bodily rhythms were declining simultaneously, as if her very life was fading.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: How? This didn't happen in the clinical trials!

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Darling! Erina! Open your eyes!
Erina half-opened her eyes and smiled faintly.

blocks.speaker: Erina
blocks.content: Darling... I'm okay.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Erina! What's wrong? Where does it hurt?

blocks.speaker: Erina
blocks.content: A rejection reaction started from the terminal nerves. There must be something different about human brains. But you've done something incredible. With a little more research...
Gah!
Erina convulsed, gasping for air.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Darling! Don't speak! I'll fix this! No matter what, I'll save you...!
Alpheas cursed his own intellect for realizing it was already irreversible.
But he had to say it.
Erina slowly shook her head.
What her husband knew, she now knew too.

blocks.speaker: Erina
blocks.content: Darling... hold my hand.
Alpheas gripped her hand tightly.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: If there had been even a 1% chance, he would have tried. But now, all he could do was stay by her side.

blocks.speaker: Erina
blocks.content: Erina, this can't be. How can this...?

blocks.speaker: Erina
blocks.content: Darling, I'm sorry.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: For what? You have nothing to—

blocks.speaker: Erina
blocks.content: For being stupid.
Alpheas's heart shattered.
How could he have forgotten her words—that she was beautiful just as she was? Why had he tried to change her?

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: No... why are you sorry? I'm the one who's sorry! I was the stupid one! I was the fool!

blocks.speaker: Erina
blocks.content: Meeting you was my greatest fortune.
Erina finally understood her husband. The wonderful world ruled by reason. He had lived there. She was happy to share it briefly, to carry his memories as she departed.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Darling! Open your eyes! Please... I was wrong, darling!
As Erina's eyes closed, Alpheas hugged her and wailed.
Waaaaah!
Akein bowed his head solemnly.
What made human brains different from animals? If they had conducted human trials from the start, this tragedy could have been avoided.
But it was a meaningless assumption.
Erina had set one condition for the experiment: never test it on others.

blocks.speaker: Akein
blocks.content: That was why he had poured his heart into it. The data gathered would serve humanity well.
I'm sorry. I won't let your sacrifice be in vain.
A dull thud sounded.
Turning, he saw Alpheas smashing his head against the wall.
Waaaaah!
Each impact produced a cracking sound—whether from the wall or his skull was unclear.
Thud! Thud! Thud!
After repeatedly bashing his head, Alpheas's body bounced back like a rubber ball.
But he didn't stop.
The horror of surviving alone while his wife died twisted his insides.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Arrogant Alpheas!
Alpheas charged at the wall again.
Thud!
Klump grabbed him.

blocks.speaker: Klump
blocks.content: Alpheas! Snap out of it! What are you doing?!

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Let go! Damn it! Waaaaah!
Even Klump's strength struggled to restrain him.
It was as if his soul was burning itself out in a rush toward death.
But eventually, shock took over, his pupils dilating as consciousness faded.
Erina... Erina...
Even unconscious, he called his wife's name, bringing tears to Klump's eyes.
